articles.title: Hackers exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign
articles.summary: Hackers are running a large-scale campaign to steal credentials in an automated way after exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in vulnerable Next.js apps. [...]

articles.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent implant. articles.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
articles.summary: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-35616 (CVSS score: 9.1), has been described as a pre-authentication API access bypass leading to privile

articles.title: Axios npm hack used fake Teams error fix to hijack maintainer account
articles.summary: The maintainers of the popular Axios HTTP client have published a detailed post-mortem describing how one of its developers was targeted by a social engineering campaign believed to have been conducted by North Korean threat actors. [...]

articles.title: Device code phishing attacks surge 37x as new kits spread online
articles.summary: Device code phishing attacks that abuse the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant flow to hijack accounts have surged more than 37 times this year. [...]

articles.title: European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Hackers stole over 300GB of data from the Commission’s AWS environment, including personal information. The post European Commission Confirms Data Breach Linked to Trivy Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: CISA gives agencies two weeks to patch video conferencing bug exploited by Chinese hackers
articles.summary: A bug in a popular line of video conferencing software is being exploited by hackers, prompting the U.S. government to order all agencies to patch the vulnerability within two weeks.

articles.title: EU cyber agency attributes major data breach to TeamPCP hacking group
articles.summary: The European Union’s cybersecurity agency said the hacking group TeamPCP was behind a massive recent data breach at the European Commission.

articles.title: Hims & Hers warns of data breach after Zendesk support ticket breach
articles.summary: Telehealth giant Hims & Hers Health is warning that it suffered a data breach after support tickets were stolen from a third-party customer service platform. [...]

articles.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
articles.summary: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025, following a two-year period of minimal targeting in the region. The campaign has been attributed to TA416, a cluster of activity that overlaps with DarkPeony, RedDe

articles.title: Die Linke German political party confirms data stolen by Qilin ransomware
articles.summary: The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for an attack against Die Linke ('The Left'), forcing an IT systems outage at the political party, and threatening sensitive data leak. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Details Cookie-Controlled PHP Web Shells Persisting via Cron on Linux Servers
articles.summary: Threat actors are increasingly using HTTP cookies as a control channel for PHP-based web shells on Linux servers and to achieve remote code execution, according to findings from the Microsoft Defender Security Research Team. articles.title: Evolution of Ransomware: Multi-Extortion Ransomware Attacks
articles.summary: Multi-extortion ransomware relies on stolen data to pressure victims with public leaks. Penta Security explains how its D.AMO platform keeps exfiltrated files encrypted and useless to attackers. [...]

articles.title: Massachusetts emergency communications system impacted by cyberattack
articles.summary: An emergency communications system used across a handful of small towns in northern Massachusetts was impacted by a cyberattack.

articles.title: Ukraine warns Russian hackers are revisiting past breaches to prepare new attacks
articles.summary: In a new report, CERT-UA said attackers are revisiting previously breached infrastructure to check whether access is still available, whether exploited vulnerabilities have been patched and whether previously obtained credentials remain valid.

articles.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks
articles.summary: A Chinese threat actor exploited the video conferencing platform to perform reconnaissance, escalate privileges, and execute additional payloads. The post TrueConf Zero-Day Exploited in Asian Government Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware
articles.summary: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Symantec vulnerability, anti-ClickFix mechanism added to macOS, FBI hack classified as major incident. The post In Other News: ChatGPT Data Leak, Android Rootkit, Water Facility Hit by Ransomware appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Critical ShareFile Flaws Lead to Unauthenticated RCE
articles.summary: The vulnerabilities can be chained together to bypass authentication and upload arbitrary files to the server. The post Critical ShareFile Flaws Lead to Unauthenticated RCE appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: UNC1069 Social Engineering of Axios Maintainer Led to npm Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: The maintainer of the Axios npm package has confirmed that the supply chain compromise was the result of a highly-targeted social engineering campaign orchestrated by North Korean threat actors tracked as UNC1069. articles.title: Why Third-Party Risk Is the Biggest Gap in Your Clients' Security Posture
articles.summary: The next major breach hitting your clients probably won't come from inside their walls. articles.title: Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control
articles.summary: Shadow AI embedded in everyday apps, combined with outdated mobile devices and zero-click exploits, is creating a new and largely unseen mobile risk. The post Mobile Attack Surface Expands as Enterprises Lose Control appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign
articles.summary: Using automated scanning and the Nexus Listener collection framework, the hackers compromised over 750 systems. The post React2Shell Exploited in Large-Scale Credential Harvesting Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: T-Mobile Sets the Record Straight on Latest Data Breach Filing
articles.summary: The cybersecurity incident involved an insider and had a limited impact, the telecoms giant told SecurityWeek. The post T-Mobile Sets the Record Straight on Latest Data Breach Filing appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Drain $285 Million From Drift in 10 Seconds
articles.summary: The attackers prepared infrastructure and multiple nonce-based transactions, took over an admin key, and drained five vaults. The post North Korean Hackers Drain $285 Million From Drift in 10 Seconds appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Solana-based decentralized exchange Drift has confirmed that attackers drained about $285 million from the platform during a security incident that took place on April 1, 2026. articles.title: CERT-EU: European Commission hack exposes data of 30 EU entities
articles.summary: The European Union's Cybersecurity Service (CERT-EU) has attributed the European Commission cloud hack to the TeamPCP threat group, saying the resulting breach exposed the data of at least 29 other Union entities. [...]

articles.title: Drift loses $280 million as North Korean hackers seize Security Council powers
articles.summary: The Drift Protocol lost at least $280 million after a threat actor took control of its Security Council administrative powers in a planned, sophisticated operation. [...]

articles.title: Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak
articles.summary: Within days of each other, Anthropic first leaked the source code to Claude Code, and then a critical vulnerability was found by Adversa AI. The post Critical Vulnerability in Claude Code Emerges Days After Source Leak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices
articles.summary: The DarkSword exploit kit has been used by both state-sponsored hackers and commercial spyware vendors. The post Apple Rolls Out DarkSword Exploit Protection to More Devices appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Residential proxies evaded IP reputation checks in 78% of 4B sessions
articles.summary: Researchers warn that residential proxies used to route malicious traffic are a big problem for IP reputation systems, as there is no clear distinction between attackers and legitimate users. [...]

articles.summary: Cisco has released updates to address a critical security flaw in the Integrated Management Controller (IMC) that, if successfully exploited, could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and gain access to the system with elevated privileges. articles.summary: From its GitHub repo: "Vite (French word for "quick", pronounced /vi?t/, like "veet") is a new breed of frontend build tooling that significantly improves the frontend development experience" [https://github.com/vitejs/vite].

articles.title: Adversaries Exploit Vacant Homes to Intercept Mail in Hybrid Cybercrime
articles.summary: Threat actors are exploiting vacant homes as "drop addresses" to intercept mail and enable fraud. Flare shows how postal services and fake identities are abused to turn mail into a fraud vector. [...]

articles.title: New Progress ShareFile flaws can be chained in pre-auth RCE attacks
articles.summary: Two vulnerabilities in Progress ShareFile, an enterprise-grade secure file transfer solution, can be chained to enable unauthenticated file exfiltration from affected environments. [...]
